title: Meta Restructuring And 2Africa Pause Weigh On AI Spending Outlook
content:
Find your next quality investment with Simply Wall St's easy and powerful screener, trusted by over 7 million individual investors worldwide.

Meta Platforms (NasdaqGS:META) is reported to be considering workforce cuts that could affect up to 20% of employees as it manages rising AI infrastructure costs.

The company is expanding AI efforts, including superintelligence labs and custom chips, which are contributing to higher capital expenditure.

Meta and its partners have paused progress on the 2Africa subsea cable project because of geopolitical conflict in parts of the planned route.

Meta Platforms, trading at around $613.71, has seen mixed share price performance, with a 1.2% return over the past year and strong gains over three years and five years. In the shorter term, the stock has recorded a 4.8% decline over the past week and an 8.2% decline over the past month, alongside a 5.6% decline year to date.

For investors, the reported restructuring and the pause in 2Africa highlight how Meta is balancing heavy AI spending with cost controls and operational risk. The way the company manages these shifts could influence its capacity to fund AI projects, retain key talent, and expand connectivity in high growth regions over time.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Meta Platforms by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Meta Platforms.

We've flagged 0 risks for Meta Platforms. See which could impact your investment.

✅ Price vs Analyst Target: At US$613.71 versus a US$862.25 analyst price target, the share price sits about 29% below consensus.

✅ Simply Wall St Valuation: Simply Wall St estimates the stock is trading 45.4% below its fair value, flagged as undervalued.

❌ Recent Momentum: The 30 day return of an 8.2% decline shows weak short term momentum as the restructuring news lands.

There is only one way to know the right time to buy, sell or hold Meta Platforms. Head to Simply Wall St's company report for the latest analysis of Meta Platforms's Fair Value..

📊 Workforce cuts and the 2Africa pause suggest management is prioritizing funding for AI infrastructure while trimming other cost and project commitments.

📊 Keep an eye on capital expenditure, AI related returns and any commentary on how restructuring affects productivity and product roadmaps.

⚠️ The biggest watchpoint is execution risk, if cuts hurt key AI talent or geopolitical tensions cause longer interruptions to connectivity projects.

For the full picture including more risks and rewards, check out the complete Meta Platforms analysis. Alternatively, you can check out the community page for Meta Platforms to see how other investors believe this latest news will impact the company's narrative.

title: SAIC Earnings: What To Look For From SAIC
content:
Government IT services provider Science Applications International Corporation (NASDAQ:SAIC) will be reporting results this Monday morning. Here’s what to look for.

SAIC met analysts’ revenue expectations last quarter, reporting revenues of $1.87 billion, down 5.6% year on year. It was a very strong quarter for the company, with a beat of analysts’ EPS estimates and an impressive beat of analysts’ full-year EPS guidance estimates.

Is SAIC a buy or sell going into earnings? Read our full analysis here, it’s free for active Edge members.

This quarter, the market is expecting SAIC’s revenue to decline 3.8% year on year, a reversal from the 5.8% increase it recorded in the same quarter last year.

Analysts covering the company have generally reconfirmed their estimates over the last 30 days, suggesting they anticipate the business to stay the course heading into earnings. SAIC has missed Wall Street’s revenue estimates multiple times over the last two years.

Looking at SAIC’s peers in the government & technical consulting segment, some have already reported their Q4 results, giving us a hint as to what we can expect. Jacobs Solutions delivered year-on-year revenue growth of 8.2%, beating analysts’ expectations by 1.7%, and ICF International reported a revenue decline of 10.6%, topping estimates by 1%. Jacobs Solutions traded up 7.8% following the results while ICF International was also up 4.3%.

Read our full analysis of Jacobs Solutions’s results here and ICF International’s results here.

The outlook for 2025 remains clouded by potential trade policy changes and corporate tax discussions, which could impact business confidence and growth. While some of the government & technical consulting stocks have shown solid performance in this choppy environment, the group has generally underperformed, with share prices down 4.2% on average over the last month. SAIC is up 5.8% during the same time and is heading into earnings with an average analyst price target of $116.22 (compared to the current share price of $91.63).

title: Analysts Reframe US Foods (USFD) Story As Targets Shift And Growth Expectations Reset
content:
Find winning stocks in any market cycle. Join 7 million investors using Simply Wall St's investing ideas for FREE.

The latest update pegs fair value for US Foods Holding at US$108.87, a small move from the prior US$110.20 target. This sits against a backdrop of analysts clustering around higher valuation ranges, with some lifting targets into the high US$80s to mid US$90s and others striking a more cautious tone on how much is already reflected in the share price. Read on to see how you can follow this evolving narrative and what it might mean for your own view on the stock.

Analyst Price Targets don't always capture the full story. Head over to our Company Report to find new ways to value US Foods Holding.

Morgan Stanley, Citi, UBS, Bernstein, Deutsche Bank, BMO Capital, BTIG, Guggenheim and Barclays have all issued research that includes higher price targets for US Foods Holding, which points to a generally supportive stance on the shares.

Morgan Stanley described Q4 as a down the middle print in a mixed quarter and still raised its target to US$94 while keeping an Equal Weight rating, suggesting the current execution is broadly in line with its expectations.

Several firms, including Guggenheim, Citi and others, have made sizeable upward revisions to their targets, indicating they see room for value creation relative to their prior views.

Citi has maintained a Buy rating even after trimming its target to US$90 in January, and has stated that it views food distributors as better positioned than restaurants within its 2026 outlook.

Piper Sandler recently downgraded US Foods, signaling concern about how much optimism may already be reflected in the share price or about the balance between risk and reward at current levels.

Morgan Stanley’s Equal Weight rating, despite a higher target, shows that not all analysts see a clear edge versus the broader market, which can matter if you are comparing US Foods to other options in the sector.

Do your thoughts align with the Bull or Bear Analysts? Perhaps you think there's more to the story. Head to the Simply Wall St Community to discover more perspectives!

We've flagged 2 risks for US Foods Holding. See which could impact your investment.

US Foods launched Menu IQ, an AI powered tool that helps restaurant operators track real time profitability by menu item and manage food costs using integrated data from the MOXe platform, offered at no additional cost to customers.

The company completed repurchases of 10,862,649 shares, representing 4.77% of shares, for a total of US$908.29m under its buyback program announced on May 8, 2025.

Management signaled interest in tuck in M&A to increase local market density, with an emphasis on adding distribution centers in areas where it does not yet have a location or where demand is growing faster.

US Foods reaffirmed its 2025 earnings guidance with expected net sales growth of 4% to 5%, and provided 2026 guidance with expected net sales growth of 4% to 6%.

Fair value has been updated to US$108.87 from US$110.20.

Revenue growth is now set at 4.96% from 4.97%.

The net profit margin has been updated to 2.70% from 2.72%.

The future P/E is now 20.97x, compared with 21.04x previously.

The discount rate has been adjusted to 7.40% from 7.43%.

Narratives connect a company’s business story to the assumptions behind its forecasts and fair value, so you can see what is driving the numbers. They refresh as new earnings, news, and analyst views come through.

Head over to the Simply Wall St Community and follow the Narrative on US Foods Holding to stay up to date on:

How investments in digital platforms like MOXe, automation, and tools such as Menu IQ are being used to improve efficiency, margins, and customer experience.

The role of private label and value added offerings, sustainability credentials, and specialized delivery solutions like Pronto in supporting revenue and earnings growth.

Key risks around softer food away from home trends, potential limits to cost savings, regulatory and ESG requirements, and execution challenges in any larger M&A moves.

title: How InMode's New 10% Share Buyback Plan Will Impact InMode (INMD) Investors
content:
On March 13, 2026, InMode Ltd. announced that its Board of Directors approved a share repurchase program for up to 6,380,000 shares, or 10% of its outstanding stock, to be funded with existing cash.

This fresh authorization, coming after the company previously spent about US$127.4 million on buybacks in 2025, highlights management’s ongoing emphasis on returning capital to shareholders alongside other capital allocation options.

With this new buyback authorization now in place, we’ll explore how committing cash to retire up to 10% of shares reshapes InMode’s investment narrative.

Rare earth metals are an input to most high-tech devices, military and defence systems and electric vehicles. The global race is on to secure supply of these critical minerals. Beat the pack to uncover the 30 best rare earth metal stocks of the very few that mine this essential strategic resource.

To own InMode today, you need to believe that demand for minimally invasive aesthetic treatments and new wellness platforms can support profitable growth despite softer equipment spending and margin pressure. The new authorization to repurchase up to 10% of shares does not materially change the near term demand outlook or the key risk that prolonged macro headwinds and weaker capital purchases could keep recent revenue and earnings under pressure.

The fresh buyback plan sits alongside mixed 2025 results, where full year sales were about US$370.5 million and net income was about US$93.8 million, down from the prior year. That earnings reset keeps execution on new platforms in areas like urology and ophthalmology, and any future regulatory progress, firmly in focus as potential offsets to slower core aesthetic equipment demand.

Yet behind the headlines of a 10% buyback, investors should also be aware of the growing risk that shifting procedure trends and lower priced noninvasive treatments could...

Read the full narrative on InMode (it's free!)

InMode's narrative projects $409.0 million revenue and $94.2 million earnings by 2028.

Uncover how InMode's forecasts yield a $16.25 fair value, a 20% upside to its current price.

Some of the most optimistic analysts were expecting revenue of about US$474.5 million and earnings of about US$125.3 million before this buyback news, so if you are weighing that more upbeat view against the risk of fiercer competition and slower approvals for new indications, it is worth asking how this fresh plan to retire up to 10 percent of the share count might tilt those projections in either direction.
